# Layer Grammar Planning Note
Date: 2026-04-19T12:23:45.3444339-04:00
Project: AIVA_CORE
Classification: semi_private
Status: captured

## Planning Relevance
The visual work and real-world build history both reinforce that the system should think about layers as more than technical levels.

A layer may function as:
- a role
- a segment
- an anchor
- an emotion
- an energy band
- a message band
- a boundary
- a workflow surface

## Planning Implication
The system should preserve that:
- segmented structures can hold distinct meaning
- repeated signal can create continuity
- anchors can stabilize surrounding complexity
- one environment can contain multiple bounded roles without losing unified meaning
- workflow is what activates layers

## Current Coding Relevance
This note supports:
- governed memory runtime
- layered planning language
- future retrieval grammar
- continuity objects that preserve design intent, not only technical state

## Tightest Line
Whole system, bounded sections, distinct roles, unified meaning.
